title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 10
subtitle: Hard boundary-testing paper covering 2026 syllabus topics skipped in 2023

metadata.strategy: Deliberately includes hard questions on syllabus topics the 2023 examiner skipped: averages, trigonometry, heights and distances, data interpretation, surds, Venn diagrams, statement-conclusion, error spotting, voice, narration, IT Act, digital signatures and e-governance.

instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.
This surprise paper stretches to the edges of the official syllabus at a hard difficulty tier.

questions.text: The average of 11 results is 50. The average of the first six is 49 and of the last six is 52. Enter the sixth result.
A batsman scores 92 in his 17th innings, thereby increasing his average by 2 runs. His average after 17 innings is:
The value of sin^2(30) + cos^2(45) + tan^2(60) is:
If tan(theta) + cot(theta) = 2, then tan^2(theta) + cot^2(theta) equals:
From the top of a 60 m building, the angles of depression of the top and bottom of a tower are 30 and 60 degrees respectively. The height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: Distance = 20 sqrt(3); 60 - 20 sqrt(3) x tan(30) = 40.

questions.text: A company's sales (Rs lakh) were: 2021: 32, 2022: 40, 2023: 50, 2024: 65. The percentage increase from 2023 to 2024 is:
Using the same data (2021: 32, 2022: 40, 2023: 50, 2024: 65), the ratio of combined sales of 2021-22 to combined sales of 2023-24 is:
The square root of 0.000441 is:
Arrange in ascending order: sqrt(2), cube root of 3, fourth root of 5.

options.text: sqrt(2) < cube root of 3 < fourth root of 5
fourth root of 5 < cube root of 3 < sqrt(2)
cube root of 3 < sqrt(2) < fourth root of 5
sqrt(2) < fourth root of 5 < cube root of 3

questions.explanation: Raise all to the 12th power: 64, 81, 125.

questions.text: The value of (sqrt(5)+sqrt(3))/(sqrt(5)-sqrt(3)) + (sqrt(5)-sqrt(3))/(sqrt(5)+sqrt(3)) is:
Fresh fruit contains 68% water and dried fruit contains 20% water. How much dried fruit can be obtained from 100 kg of fresh fruit?
questions.explanation: 32 kg of solids form 80% of the dried fruit: 40 kg.

metadata.topic: Profit & Loss (Gain in Terms of SP)

questions.text: By selling 33 metres of cloth, a trader gains an amount equal to the selling price of 11 metres. His gain percentage is:
questions.explanation: SP of 33 - CP of 33 = SP of 11, so CP33 = SP22; gain 50%.

questions.text: A bus covers 60% of a journey at 60 km/h and the remaining 40% at 40 km/h. Its average speed for the whole journey is:
questions.explanation: Time = 0.01D + 0.01D; average = D/0.02D = 50.

questions.text: The difference between compound interest and simple interest on a sum for 3 years at 10% per annum is Rs 62. The sum is:
The interior and exterior angles of a regular polygon are in the ratio 7:2. The number of sides of the polygon is:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = 40 degrees; 360/40 = 9.

questions.text: A right pyramid has a square base of side 10 cm and height 12 cm. Its lateral surface area is:
questions.explanation: Slant height = 13; LSA = (1/2) x 40 x 13 = 260.

questions.text: Spot the part with the error: (a) Being a rainy day / (b) we decided / (c) to stay indoors. / (d) No error
questions.explanation: The correct form is 'It being a rainy day' (absolute construction).

questions.text: Spot the part with the error: (a) The teacher as well as the students / (b) were present / (c) at the seminar. / (d) No error
questions.explanation: With 'as well as', the verb agrees with the first subject: 'was'.

questions.text: The lawyer prevailed ____ his client to settle the dispute out of court.
questions.explanation: 'Prevail upon' means to persuade.

questions.text: The officer was accused ____ accepting bribes.
questions.explanation: 'Accused of' is the correct collocation.

questions.text: Change to passive voice: 'Who taught you French?'

options.text: By whom were you taught French?
By whom French was taught to you?
Who was French taught by you?
French was taught you by whom?

questions.explanation: The interrogative takes 'By whom were you taught...'.

questions.text: Change to indirect speech: He said to me, 'Do you know the way to the station?'

options.text: He asked me whether I knew the way to the station.
He asked me did I know the way to the station.
He told me whether I know the way to the station.
He asked me that I knew the way to the station.

questions.explanation: Yes/no questions convert with 'whether/if' and backshift.

questions.text: Rearrange the parts to form a meaningful sentence: P: by the sudden collapse of the footbridge / Q: which had stood for over a century / R: the morning commuters were stranded / S: on both banks of the swollen river
questions.explanation: The commuters were stranded by the collapse of the bridge, which..., on both banks.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: UNLESS YOU DON'T WORK hard, you will fail.
questions.explanation: 'Unless' already carries the negative sense.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To take something with a pinch of salt
questions.explanation: It means to be sceptical about a claim.

questions.text: A formal speech or writing praising someone highly:
questions.explanation: A panegyric is elaborate public praise.

questions.text: Select the synonym of SAGACIOUS:
questions.explanation: Sagacious means keenly discerning and wise.

questions.text: Select the antonym of IGNOMINY:
questions.explanation: Ignominy (public shame) is opposite to honour.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The French loanword is spelt rendezvous.

questions.passage: India's digital public infrastructure, the trio of Aadhaar identity, UPI payments and consent-based data-sharing frameworks, has been praised abroad as a model for inclusive growth. Services that once demanded days of queuing, from opening a bank account to receiving a welfare payment, now conclude in minutes. Yet scholars caution that the same rails that deliver subsidies can also exclude: an authentication failure at a ration shop may deny food to those who need it most, and every transaction leaves a data trail that invites surveillance. The lesson, they argue, is that digital systems must be designed with error tolerance and privacy safeguards, not merely for speed and scale.
questions.text: According to the passage, India's digital public infrastructure has been praised mainly because it:
questions.explanation: It is cited abroad as a model for fast, inclusive delivery.

questions.passage: India's digital public infrastructure, the trio of Aadhaar identity, UPI payments and consent-based data-sharing frameworks, has been praised abroad as a model for inclusive growth. Services that once demanded days of queuing, from opening a bank account to receiving a welfare payment, now conclude in minutes. Yet scholars caution that the same rails that deliver subsidies can also exclude: an authentication failure at a ration shop may deny food to those who need it most, and every transaction leaves a data trail that invites surveillance. The lesson, they argue, is that digital systems must be designed with error tolerance and privacy safeguards, not merely for speed and scale.
questions.text: The scholars' caution described in the passage concerns:

options.text: The high cost of maintaining servers
Exclusion through authentication failures and surveillance through data trails
The slow speed of digital payments

questions.explanation: They warn of exclusion errors and privacy risks.

questions.passage: India's digital public infrastructure, the trio of Aadhaar identity, UPI payments and consent-based data-sharing frameworks, has been praised abroad as a model for inclusive growth. Services that once demanded days of queuing, from opening a bank account to receiving a welfare payment, now conclude in minutes. Yet scholars caution that the same rails that deliver subsidies can also exclude: an authentication failure at a ration shop may deny food to those who need it most, and every transaction leaves a data trail that invites surveillance. The lesson, they argue, is that digital systems must be designed with error tolerance and privacy safeguards, not merely for speed and scale.
questions.text: In the passage, the word 'rails' is used metaphorically to mean:

options.text: The underlying digital systems on which services run

questions.explanation: 'Rails' refers to the shared digital infrastructure.

questions.text: Find the next term: 5, 12, 33, 68, 117, ?
questions.explanation: Differences 7, 21, 35, 49 grow by 14; next difference is 63.

questions.text: Find the next term: 4, 9, 25, 49, 121, 169, ?
questions.explanation: Squares of primes; next is 17^2 = 289.

questions.text: Find the next term: AZ, CX, FU, JQ, ?
questions.explanation: First letters +2,+3,+4,+5; second letters -2,-3,-4,-5.

questions.text: If ENGLAND is written as 1234526 and FRANCE as 785291, how is GREECE written?
If MOBILE = 56, then PHONE = ?
Which word CANNOT be formed from the letters of MEASUREMENT?
questions.explanation: REMNANT needs two Ns; MEASUREMENT has only one.

questions.text: Which diagram best represents the relationship among Professors, Men and Married People?
questions.explanation: Each pair can overlap without full containment.

questions.text: Of 120 students, 65 study Physics, 45 Chemistry and 42 Biology; 20 study Physics and Chemistry, 25 Physics and Biology, 15 Chemistry and Biology, and 8 study all three. How many study ONLY Physics?
If a ∆ b = a + b - 2, then the value of (3 ∆ 5) ∆ 4 is:
Select the option EXACTLY identical to: 14-08-2025 / Bengaluru - 560012
questions.explanation: The others alter the year, PIN or city spelling.

questions.text: Five people A, B, C, D, E sit in a row facing north. Exactly two people sit between A and D. B is immediately to the right of A. E is not adjacent to D, and C sits second from the left. Who sits exactly in the middle?
questions.explanation: The only valid order is D, C, E, A, B.

questions.text: Six subjects are scheduled Monday to Saturday, one per day. English is on Wednesday and Hindi on Saturday. Maths is the day immediately after Biology, and Chemistry is the day immediately after English. Physics is not on Monday. On which day is Physics?
questions.explanation: Bio-Maths must take Mon-Tue; Chem Thu; Physics Friday.

questions.text: A's mother is the sister of B and the daughter of C. D is the daughter of B and the sister of E. How is C related to E?
questions.explanation: C is the parent of B, and E is B's child; C's gender is unknown.

questions.text: Riya walks 5 km north, then turns right and walks 12 km. How far is she from her starting point?
Statement: An advertisement says, 'Double your money in 24 months - invest in Scheme X.' Conclusions: I. Scheme X legally guarantees the returns. II. People wish to multiply their money.
questions.explanation: Ads appeal to a desire; a claim is not a legal guarantee.

questions.text: Statement: 'If it rains, the match will be shifted indoors.' It did not rain. Conclusions: I. The match was held outdoors. II. The match was cancelled.
questions.explanation: The statement says nothing about what happens without rain.

questions.text: A city's average AQI was 180 in December but 95 in July. Which conclusion is best supported?

options.text: Air quality in this city worsens in winter
The city has the worst air in the country

questions.explanation: The data shows a seasonal worsening, nothing more.

questions.text: Statements: All pens are erasers. Some erasers are sharpeners. No sharpener is a scale. Conclusions: I. Some erasers are not scales. II. Some pens are sharpeners.
questions.explanation: Eraser-sharpeners cannot be scales; II is uncertain.

questions.text: A 4 cm cube painted on all faces is cut into 1 cm cubes. How many small cubes have paint on exactly two faces?
questions.explanation: 12 edges x (4-2) = 24 edge cubes.

questions.text: A square sheet is folded twice, once vertically and once diagonally, and a hole is punched. Select how the sheet appears when unfolded.

figure.label: Punched Hole Pattern
figure.description: A double-folded sheet with one punch; the unfolded symmetric hole pattern must be chosen from four options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual punching question.

questions.text: Select the complex figure in which the given simple figure is embedded in its original orientation.

figure.label: Embedded Figure
figure.description: A simple polygon that must be located, unrotated, inside one of four dense complex figures.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual embedded-figure question.

questions.text: Figure A relates to figure B by rotation and inversion of shading. Select the figure that relates to C in the same way.

figure.label: Figural Analogy
figure.description: A pair of related figures whose rotation-and-shading rule must be applied to a third figure; four candidate answers.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual analogy question.

questions.text: Sound travels fastest through which of the following media?
questions.explanation: Sound is fastest in dense elastic solids like steel.

questions.text: Deficiency of iodine in the diet most commonly causes:
questions.explanation: Iodine deficiency enlarges the thyroid (goitre).

questions.text: The Genome India Project, whose milestone was announced in early 2025, completed the sequencing of how many Indian genomes?
questions.explanation: 10,000 genomes from diverse Indian populations were sequenced.

questions.text: Which is the correct order of words in the Preamble describing the Republic of India?
questions.explanation: The order is Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic.

questions.text: Abolition of untouchability is provided under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability.

questions.text: Dholavira, a UNESCO-listed Harappan city known for its water management, is located in:
questions.explanation: Dholavira lies in the Rann of Kutch, Gujarat.

questions.text: Cheraw, the bamboo dance performed between clapping bamboo staves, belongs to:
questions.explanation: Cheraw is Mizoram's traditional bamboo dance.

questions.text: The Sir Creek boundary dispute is between India and:
questions.explanation: Sir Creek lies between Gujarat and Sindh.

questions.text: 'Core inflation' is headline inflation excluding the prices of:
questions.explanation: Volatile food and fuel prices are stripped out.

questions.text: The 'angel tax', abolished in the July 2024 Budget, was levied on:

options.text: Share premiums received by startups above fair value

questions.explanation: It taxed startup share issues above fair market value.

questions.text: The COP16 summit on biological diversity (October 2024) was hosted by:
questions.explanation: Colombia hosted the biodiversity COP in Cali.

questions.text: 'Yudh Abhyas' is a joint military exercise between India and:
questions.explanation: It is an annual India-US army exercise.

questions.text: The headquarters of SAARC is located in:
questions.explanation: The SAARC Secretariat is in Kathmandu, Nepal.

questions.text: The BharatNet project aims to provide high-speed broadband connectivity to:
questions.explanation: It links gram panchayats with optical fibre.

questions.text: 'Kavach', developed by Indian Railways, is a system for:
questions.explanation: Kavach automatically brakes trains to prevent collisions.

questions.text: 'The White Tiger', which won the Booker Prize in 2008, was written by:
questions.explanation: Adiga won for his debut novel.

questions.text: Section 66A of the IT Act, which criminalised offensive online messages, was struck down by the Supreme Court in 2015 in which case?

options.text: Shreya Singhal v. Union of India
K. S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India

questions.explanation: Shreya Singhal (2015) invalidated Section 66A.

questions.text: In public-key cryptography, a digital signature is created using:
questions.explanation: The private key signs; the public key verifies.

questions.text: Under the IT Act, licences to Certifying Authorities that issue digital signature certificates are granted by the:
questions.explanation: The CCA licenses and regulates Certifying Authorities.

questions.text: The UMANG mobile app is designed to provide:

options.text: Unified access to multiple government services

questions.explanation: UMANG bundles central and state e-services in one app.

questions.text: Data sharing between two government ministries through e-Office is an example of which e-Governance model?
questions.explanation: Government-to-Government interaction.

questions.text: The maximum size of a single file on a FAT32 file system is:
questions.explanation: FAT32 caps individual files at 4 GB.

questions.text: MICR technology, which reads the code line on cheques, is chiefly used by:
questions.explanation: Magnetic Ink Character Recognition processes cheques.

questions.text: In Excel, what does =POWER(2,10) return?
questions.explanation: 2 raised to the 10th power is 1024.

questions.text: A file with the extension .potx is a PowerPoint:
questions.explanation: .potx stores a presentation template.

questions.text: The village-level centres that deliver e-governance services to citizens under the Digital India programme are called:
questions.explanation: CSCs act as rural information and service kiosks.
